label: concave
prose: \(h\) is strictly concave on \([0,\infty )\).

label: log-sum
prose: If \(S\) is a finite set, and \(a_s,b_s\) are non-negative for \(s\in S\), then \[ \sum _{s\in S} a_s \log \frac{a_s}{b_s}\ge \left(\sum _{s\in S}a_s\right)\log \frac{\sum _{s\in S} a_s}{\sum _{s\in S} b_s}, \] with the convention \(0\log \frac{0}{b}=0\) for any \(b\ge 0\) and \(0\log \frac{a}{0}=\infty \) for any \(a{\gt}0\).

label: converse-log-sum
prose: If equality holds in Lemma 1.2 , then \(a_s=r\cdot b_s\) for every \(s\in S\), for some constant \(r\in \mathbb {R}\).

label: entropy-def
prose: If \(X\) is an \(S\)-valued random variable, the entropy \(\mathbb {H}[X]\) of \(X\) is defined \[ \mathbb {H}[X] := \sum _{s \in S} \mathbb {P}[X=x] \log \frac{1}{\mathbb {P}[X=x]} \] with the convention that \(0 \log \frac{1}{0} = 0\).

label: relabeled-entropy
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\) and \(Y: \Omega \to T\) are random variables, and \(Y = f(X)\) for some injection \(f: S \to T\), then \(\mathbb {H}[X] = \mathbb {H}[Y]\). If \(X: \Omega \to S\) and \(Y: \Omega \to T\) are random variables, and \(Y = f(X)\) and \(X = g(Y)\) for some functions \(f: S \to T\), \(g: T \to S\), then \(\mathbb {H}[X] = \mathbb {H}[Y]\).

label: jensen-bound
prose: If \(X\) is an \(S\)-valued random variable, then \(\mathbb {H}[X] \leq \log |S|\).

label: uniform-def
prose: If \(H\) is a subset of \(S\), an \(S\)-random variable \(X\) is said to be uniformly distributed on \(H\) if \(\mathbb {P}[X = s] = 1/|H|\) for \(s \in X\) and \(\mathbb {P}[X=s] = 0\) otherwise.

label: unif-exist
prose: Given a finite non-empty subset \(H\) of a set \(S\), there exists a random variable \(X\) (on some probability space) that is uniformly distributed on \(H\).

label: uniform-entropy
prose: If \(X\) is \(S\)-valued random variable, then \(\mathbb {H}[X] = \log |S|\) if and only if \(X\) is uniformly distributed on \(S\).

label: uniform-entropy-II
prose: If \(X\) is uniformly distributed on \(H\), then, then \(\mathbb {H}[X] = \log |H|\).

label: bound-conc
prose: If \(X\) is an \(S\)-valued random variable, then there exists \(s \in S\) such that \(\mathbb {P}[X=s] \geq \exp (-\mathbb {H}[X])\).

label: entropy-comm
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\), \(Y: \Omega \to T\), and \(Z: \Omega \to U\) are random variables, then \(\mathbb {H}[X, Y] = \mathbb {H}[Y, X]\) and \(\mathbb {H}[X, (Y,Z)] = \mathbb {H}[(X,Y), Z]\).

label: condition-event-def
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\) is an \(S\)-valued random variable and \(E\) is an event in \(\Omega \), then the conditioned event \((X|E)\) is defined to be the same random variable as \(X\), but now the ambient probability measure has been conditioned to \(E\).

label: conditional-entropy-def
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\) and \(Y: \Omega \to T\) are random variables, the conditional entropy \(\mathbb {H}[X|Y]\) is defined as \[ \mathbb {H}[X|Y] := \sum _{y \in Y} \mathbb {P}[Y = y] \mathbb {H}[(X | Y=y)]. \]

label: relabeled-entropy-cond
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\), \(Y: \Omega \to T\), and \(Z: \Omega \to U\) are random variables, and \(Y = f(X,Z)\) almost surely for some map \(f: S \times U \to T\) that is injective for each fixed \(U\), then \(\mathbb {H}[X|Z] = \mathbb {H}[Y|Z]\). Similarly, if \(g: T \to U\) is injective, then \(\mathbb {H}[X|g(Y)] = \mathbb {H}[X|Y]\).

label: chain-rule
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\) and \(Y: \Omega \to T\) are random variables, then \[ \mathbb {H}[X, Y] = \mathbb {H}[Y] + \mathbb {H}[X|Y]. \]

label: conditional-chain-rule
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\), \(Y: \Omega \to T\), \(Z: \Omega \to U\) are random variables, then \[ \mathbb {H}[X, Y | Z] = \mathbb {H}[Y | Z] + \mathbb {H}[X|Y, Z]. \]

label: information-def
prose: If \(X: \Omega \to S\), \(Y: \Omega \to T\) are random variables, then \[ \mathbb {I}[X : Y] := \mathbb {H}[X] + \mathbb {H}[Y] - \mathbb {H}[X,Y]. \]

label: alternative-mutual
prose: With notation as above, we have \[ \mathbb {I}[X : Y] = \mathbb {I}[Y:X] \] \[ \mathbb {I}[X : Y] = \mathbb {H}[X] - \mathbb {H}[X|Y] \] \[ \mathbb {I}[X : Y] = \mathbb {H}[Y] - \mathbb {H}[Y|X] \]

label: mutual-nonneg
prose: We have \(\mathbb {I}[X:Y] \geq 0\).

label: subadditive
prose: With notation as above, we have \(\mathbb {H}[X,Y] \leq \mathbb {H}[X] + \mathbb {H}[Y]\).

label: cond-reduce
prose: With notation as above, we have \(\mathbb {H}[X|Y] \leq \mathbb {H}[X]\).

label: submodularity
prose: With three random variables \(X,Y,Z\), one has \(\mathbb {H}[X|Y,Z] \leq \mathbb {H}[X|Z]\).

label: alt-submodularity
prose: With three random variables \(X,Y,Z\), one has \[ \mathbb {H}[X,Y,Z] + \mathbb {H}[Z] \leq \mathbb {H}[X,Z] + \mathbb {H}[Y,Z]. \]

label: independent-def
prose: Two random variables \(X: \Omega \to S\) and \(Y: \Omega \to T\) are independent if the law of \((X,Y)\) is the product of the law of \(X\) and the law of \(Y\). Similarly for more than two variables.

label: vanish-entropy
prose: If \(X,Y\) are random variables, then \(\mathbb {I}[X:Y] = 0\) if and only if \(X,Y\) are independent.

label: add-entropy
prose: If \(X,Y\) are random variables, then \(\mathbb {H}[X,Y] = \mathbb {H}[X] + \mathbb {H}[Y]\) if and only if \(X,Y\) are independent.

label: conditional-mutual-def
prose: If \(X,Y,Z\) are random variables, with \(Z\) \(U\)-valued, then \[ \mathbb {I}[X:Y|Z] := \sum _{z \in U} P[Z=z] \mathbb {I}[(X|Z=z): (Y|Z=z)]. \]

label: conditional-mutual-alt
prose: We have \[ \mathbb {I}[X:Y|Z] := \mathbb {H}[X|Z] + \mathbb {H}[Y|Z] - \mathbb {H}[X,Y|Z] \] and \[ \mathbb {I}[X:Y|Z] := \mathbb {H}[X|Z] - \mathbb {H}[X|Y,Z]. \]

label: conditional-nonneg
prose: If \(X,Y,Z\) are random variables, then \(\mathbb {I}[X:Y|Z] \ge 0\).

label: conditional-independent-def
prose: Two random variables \(X: \Omega \to S\) and \(Y: \Omega \to T\) are conditionally independent relative to another random variable \(Z: \Omega \to U\) if \(P[X = s \wedge Y = t| Z=u] = P[X=s|Z=u] P[Y=t|Z=u]\) for all \(s \in S, t \in T, u \in U\). (We won’t need conditional independence for more variables than this.)

label: conditional-vanish
prose: If \(X,Y,Z\) are random variables, then \(\mathbb {I}[X:Y|Z] = 0\) iff \(X,Y\) are conditionally independent over \(Z\).

label: cond-trial-ent
prose: If \(X, Y\) are conditionally independent over \(Z\), then \[ \mathbb {H}[X,Y,Z] =\mathbb {H}[X,Z] + \mathbb {H}[Y,Z] - \mathbb {H}[Z]. \]

label: neg-ent
prose: If \(X\) is \(G\)-valued, then \(\mathbb {H}[-X]=\mathbb {H}[X]\).

label: shear-ent
prose: If \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued, then \(\mathbb {H}[X \pm Y | Y]=\mathbb {H}[X|Y]\) and \(\mathbb {H}[X \pm Y, Y] = \mathbb {H}[X, Y]\).

label: sumset-lower-gen
prose: If \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables on \(\Omega \), we have \[ \max (\mathbb {H}[X], \mathbb {H}[Y]) - \mathbb {I}[X:Y] \leq \mathbb {H}[X \pm Y]. \]

label: sumset-lower-gen-cond
prose: If \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables on \(\Omega \) and \(Z\) is another random variable on \(\Omega \) then \[ \max (\mathbb {H}[X|Z], \mathbb {H}[Y|Z]) - \mathbb {I}[X:Y|Z] \leq \mathbb {H}[X\pm Y|Z], \]

label: sumset-lower
prose: If \(X,Y\) are independent \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ \max (\mathbb {H}[X], \mathbb {H}[Y]) \leq \mathbb {H}[X\pm Y]. \]

label: copy-ent
prose: If \(X'\) is a copy of \(X\) then \(\mathbb {H}[X'] = \mathbb {H}[X]\).

label: independent-exist
prose: Let \(X_i : \Omega _i \to S_i\) be random variables for \(i=1,\dots ,k\). Then if one gives \(\prod _{i=1}^k S_i\) the product measure of the laws of \(X_i\), the coordinate functions \((x_j)_{j=1}^k \mapsto x_i\) are jointly independent random variables which are copies of the \(X_1,\dots ,X_k\).

label: ruz-dist-def
prose: Let \(X,Y\) be \(G\)-valued random variables (not necessarily on the same sample space). The Ruzsa distance \(d[X ;Y]\) between \(X\) and \(Y\) is defined to be \[ d[X ;Y] := \mathbb {H}[X' - Y'] - \mathbb {H}[X']/2 - \mathbb {H}[Y']/2 \] where \(X',Y'\) are (the canonical) independent copies of \(X,Y\) from Lemma 3.7 .

label: dist-zero
prose: If \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable and \(0\) is the random variable taking the value \(0\) everywhere then \[ d[X;0]=\mathbb {H}(X)/2. \]

label: ruz-copy
prose: If \(X',Y'\) are copies of \(X,Y\) respectively then \(d[X';Y']=d[X ;Y]\).

label: ruz-indep
prose: If \(X,Y\) are independent \(G\)-random variables then \[ d[X ;Y] := \mathbb {H}[X - Y] - \mathbb {H}[X]/2 - \mathbb {H}[Y]/2. \]

label: ruzsa-symm
prose: If \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ d[X ;Y] = d[Y;X]. \]

label: ruzsa-diff
prose: If \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ |\mathbb {H}[X]-H[Y]| \leq 2 d[X ;Y]. \]

label: ruzsa-growth
prose: If \(X,Y\) are independent \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ \mathbb {H}[X-Y] - \mathbb {H}[X], \mathbb {H}[X-Y] - \mathbb {H}[Y] \leq 2d[X ;Y]. \]

label: ruzsa-nonneg
prose: If \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ d[X ;Y] \geq 0. \]

label: dist-projection
prose: If \(G\) is an additive group and \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable and \(H\leq G\) is a finite subgroup then, with \(\pi :G\to G/H\) the natural homomorphism we have (where \(U_H\) is uniform on \(H\)) \[ \mathbb {H}(\pi (X))\leq 2d[X;U_H]. \]

label: ruzsa-triangle-improved
prose: If \(X,Y,Z\) are \(G\)-valued random variables on \(\Omega \) with \((X,Y)\) independent of \(Z\), then \begin{equation} \label{submod-explicit} \mathbb {H}[X - Y] \leq \mathbb {H}[X-Z] + \mathbb {H}[Z-Y] - \mathbb {H}[Z]\end{equation} 1

label: ruzsa-triangle
prose: If \(X,Y,Z\) are \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ d[X ;Y] \leq d[X ;Z] + d[Z;Y]. \]

label: cond-dist-def
prose: If \((X, Z)\) and \((Y, W)\) are random variables (where \(X\) and \(Y\) are \(G\)-valued) we define \[ d[X | Z; Y | W] := \sum _{z,w} \mathbb {P}[Z=z] \mathbb {P}[W=w] d[(X|Z=z); (Y|(W=w))]. \] similarly \[ d[X ; Y | W] := \sum _{w} \mathbb {P}[W=w] d[X ; (Y|(W=w))]. \]

label: cond-dist-alt
prose: The expression \(d[X | Z;Y | W]\) is unchanged if \((X,Z)\) or \((Y,W)\) is replaced by a copy. Furthermore, if \((X,Z)\) and \((Y,W)\) are independent, then \[ d[X | Z;Y | W] = \mathbb {H}[X-Y|Z,W] - \mathbb {H}[X|Z]/2 - \mathbb {H}[Y|W]/2 \] and similarly \[ d[X ;Y | W] = \mathbb {H}[X-Y|W] - \mathbb {H}[X]/2 - \mathbb {H}[Y|W]/2. \]

label: kv
prose: Suppose that \(X, Y, Z\) are independent \(G\)-valued random variables. Then \[ \mathbb {H}[X + Y + Z] - \mathbb {H}[X + Y] \leq \mathbb {H}[Y+ Z] - \mathbb {H}[Y]. \]

label: cond-indep-exist
prose: For \(X,Y\) random variables, there exist random variables \(X_1,X_2,Y'\) on a common probability space with \((X_1, Y'), (X_2, Y')\) both having the distribution of \((X,Y)\), and \(X_1, X_2\) conditionally independent over \(Y'\) in the sense of Definition 2.28 .

label: entropic-bsg
prose: Let \(A,B\) be \(G\)-valued random variables on \(\Omega \), and set \(Z := A+B\). Then \begin{equation} \label{2-bsg-takeaway} \sum _{z} \mathbb {P}[Z=z] d[(A | Z = z); (B | Z = z)] \leq 3 \mathbb {I}[A:B] + 2 \mathbb {H}[Z] - \mathbb {H}[A] - \mathbb {H}[B]. \end{equation} 2

label: cond-dist-fact
prose: Suppose that \((X, Z)\) and \((Y, W)\) are random variables, where \(X, Y\) take values in an abelian group. Then \[ d[X | Z;Y | W] \leq d[X ; Y] + \tfrac {1}{2} \mathbb {I}[X : Z] + \tfrac {1}{2} \mathbb {I}[Y : W]. \] In particular, \[ d[X ;Y | W] \leq d[X ; Y] + \tfrac {1}{2} \mathbb {I}[Y : W]. \]

label: first-useful
prose: Let \(X, Y, Z\) be random variables taking values in some abelian group of characteristic \(2\), and with \(Y, Z\) independent. Then we have \begin{align} \nonumber d[X ; Y + Z] -d[X ; Y] & \leq \tfrac {1}{2} (\mathbb {H}[Y+ Z] - \mathbb {H}[Y]) \\ & = \tfrac {1}{2} d[Y; Z] + \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[Z] - \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[Y]. \label{lem51-a} \end{align} and \begin{align} \nonumber d[X ;Y|Y+ Z] - d[X ;Y] & \leq \tfrac {1}{2} \bigl(\mathbb {H}[Y+ Z] - \mathbb {H}[Z]\bigr) \\ & = \tfrac {1}{2} d[Y;Z] + \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[Y] - \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[Z]. \label{ruzsa-3} \end{align}

label: second-useful
prose: Let \(X, Y, Z, Z'\) be random variables taking values in some abelian group, and with \(Y, Z, Z'\) independent. Then we have \begin{align} \nonumber & d[X ;Y + Z | Y + Z + Z’] - d[X ;Y] \\ & \qquad \leq \tfrac {1}{2} ( \mathbb {H}[Y + Z + Z’] + \mathbb {H}[Y + Z] - \mathbb {H}[Y] - \mathbb {H}[Z’]).\label{7111} \end{align}

label: sym-group-def
prose: If \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable, then the symmetry group \(\mathrm{Sym}[X]\) is the set of all \(h \in G\) such that \(X +h\) has the same distribution as \(X\).

label: sym-group
prose: If \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable, then \(\mathrm{Sym}[X]\) is a subgroup of \(G\).

label: zero-large
prose: If \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable such that \(d[X ;X]=0\), and \(x,y \in G\) are such that \(P[X=x], P[X=y]{\gt}0\), then \(x-y \in \mathrm{Sym}[X]\).

label: sym-zero
prose: If \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable with \(d[X ;X]=0\), and \(x_0\) is a point with \(P[X=x_0] {\gt} 0\), then \(X-x_0\) is uniformly distributed on \(\mathrm{Sym}[X]\).

label: lem:100pc-self
prose: Suppose that \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable such that \(d[X ;X]=0\). Then there exists a subgroup \(H \leq G\) such that \(d[X ;U_H] = 0\).

label: lem:100pc
prose: Suppose that \(X_1,X_2\) are \(G\)-valued random variables such that \(d[X_1;X_2]=0\). Then there exists a subgroup \(H \leq G\) such that \(d[X_1;U_H] = d[X_2;U_H] = 0\).

label: fibring-ident
prose: Let \(\pi : H \to H'\) be a homomorphism additive groups, and let \(Z_1,Z_2\) be \(H\)-valued random variables. Then we have \[ d[Z_1; Z_2] \geq d[\pi (Z_1);\pi (Z_2)] + d[Z_1|\pi (Z_1); Z_2 |\pi (Z_2)]. \] Moreover, if \(Z_1,Z_2\) are taken to be independent, then the difference between the two sides is \[ I( Z_1 - Z_2 : (\pi (Z_1), \pi (Z_2)) | \pi (Z_1 - Z_2) ). \]

label: fibring-ineq
prose: If \(\pi :G\to H\) is a homomorphism of additive groups and \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables then \[ d[X;Y]\geq d[\pi (X);\pi (Y)]. \]

label: cor-fibre
prose: Let \(Y_1,Y_2,Y_3\) and \(Y_4\) be independent \(G\)-valued random variables. Then \begin{align*} & d[Y_1+Y_3; Y_2+Y_4] + d[Y_1|Y_1+Y_3; Y_2|Y_2+Y_4] \\ & \qquad + \mathbb {I}[Y_1+Y_2 : Y_2 + Y_4 | Y_1+Y_2+Y_3+Y_4] = d[Y_1; Y_2] + d[Y_3; Y_4]. \end{align*}

label: tau-def
prose: If \(X_1,X_2\) are two \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ \tau [X_1; X_2] := d[X_1; X_2] + \eta d[X^0_1; X_1] + \eta d[X^0_2; X_2]. \]

label: tau-copy
prose: If \(X'_1, X'_2\) are copies of \(X_1,X_2\), then \(\tau [X'_1;X'_2] = \tau [X_1;X_2]\).

label: tau-min-def
prose: A pair of \(G\)-valued random variables \(X_1, X_2\) are said to be a \(\tau \)-minimizer if one has \[ \tau [X_1;X_2] \leq \tau [X'_1;X'_2] \] for all \(G\)-valued random variables \(X'_1, X'_2\).

label: tau-min
prose: A pair \(X_1, X_2\) of \(\tau \)-minimizers exist.

label: distance-lower
prose: For any \(G\)-valued random variables \(X'_1,X'_2\), one has \[ d[X'_1;X'_2] \geq k - \eta (d[X^0_1;X'_1] - d[X^0_1;X_1] ) - \eta (d[X^0_2;X'_2] - d[X^0_2;X_2] ). \]

label: cond-distance-lower
prose: For any \(G\)-valued random variables \(X'_1,X'_2\) and random variables \(Z,W\), one has \[ d[X'_1|Z;X'_2|W] \geq k - \eta (d[X^0_1;X'_1|Z] - d[X^0_1;X_1] ) - \eta (d[X^0_2;X'_2|W] - d[X^0_2;X_2] ). \]

label: first-fibre
prose: We have \begin{align*} & d[X_1+\tilde X_2;X_2+\tilde X_1] + d[X_1|X_1+\tilde X_2; X_2|X_2+\tilde X_1] \\ & \quad + \mathbb {I}[X_1+ X_2 : \tilde X_1 + X_2 \, |\, X_1 + X_2 + \tilde X_1 + \tilde X_2] = 2k. \end{align*}

label: first-dist-sum
prose: We have \begin{align*} d[X_1+\tilde X_2; X_2+\tilde X_1] \geq k & - \eta (d[X^0_1; X_1+\tilde X_2] - d[X^0_1; X_1]) \\ & \qquad - \eta (d[X^0_2; X_2+\tilde X_1] - d[X^0_2; X_2]) \end{align*}

label: first-cond
prose: We have \begin{align*} & d[X_1|X_1+\tilde X_2; X_2|X_2+\tilde X_1] \\ & \qquad \quad \geq k - \eta (d[X^0_1; X_1 | X_1 + \tilde X_2] - d[X^0_1; X_1]) \\ & \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad - \eta (d[X^0_2; X_2 | X_2 + \tilde X_1] - d[X^0_2; X_2]). \end{align*}

label: first-upper
prose: We have \begin{align*} d[X^0_1; X_1+\tilde X_2] - d[X^0_1; X_1] & \leq \tfrac {1}{2} k + \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_2] - \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_1]\\ d[X^0_2;X_2+\tilde X_1] - d[X^0_2; X_2] & \leq \tfrac {1}{2} k + \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_1] - \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_2], \\ d[X_1^0;X_1|X_1+\tilde X_2] - d[X_1^0;X_1] & \leq \tfrac {1}{2} k + \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_1] - \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_2] \\ d[X_2^0; X_2|X_2+\tilde X_1] - d[X_2^0; X_2] & \leq \tfrac {1}{2}k + \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_2] - \tfrac {1}{4} \mathbb {H}[X_1]. \end{align*}

label: first-estimate
prose: We have \(I_1 \leq 2 \eta k\).

label: foursum-bound
prose: With the same notation, we have \begin{equation} \label{HS-bound} \mathbb {H}[X_1+X_2+\tilde X_1+\tilde X_2] \le \tfrac {1}{2} \mathbb {H}[X_1]+\tfrac {1}{2} \mathbb {H}[X_2] + (2 + \eta ) k - I_1. \end{equation} 1

label: dist-sums
prose: We have \[ d[X_1+\tilde X_1; X_2+\tilde X_2] \geq k - \frac{\eta }{2} ( d[X_1; X_1] + d[X_2;X_2] ). \]

label: second-estimate-aux
prose: We have \[ d[X_1;X_1] + d[X_2;X_2] \leq 2 k + \frac{2(2 \eta k - I_1)}{1-\eta }. \]

label: second-estimate
prose: We have \[ I_2 \leq 2 \eta k + \frac{2 \eta (2 \eta k - I_1)}{1 - \eta }. \]

label: symm-lemma
prose: We have \[ I(U:W | S) = I(V:W | S). \]

label: uvw-s
prose: We have \[ I(U : V \, | \, S) + I(V : W \, | \, S) + I(W : U \, | \, S) \leq 6 \eta k - \frac{1 - 5 \eta }{1-\eta } (2 \eta k - I_1). \]

label: total-dist
prose: We have \begin{align*} \sum _{i=1}^2 \sum _{A\in \{ U,V,W\} } \big(d[X^0_i;A|S] & - d[X^0_i;X_i]\big) \\ & \leq (6 - 3\eta ) k + 3(2 \eta k - I_1). \end{align*}

label: key-ident
prose: We have \(U+V+W=0\).

label: construct-good-prelim
prose: One has \begin{align*} k \leq \delta + \eta (& d[X^0_1;T_1]-d[X^0_1;X_1]) + \eta (d[X^0_2;T_2]-d[X^0_2;X_2]) \\ & + \tfrac 12 \eta \mathbb {I}[T_1:T_3] + \tfrac 12 \eta \mathbb {I}[T_2:T_3]. \end{align*}

label: construct-good
prose: One has \begin{align*} k & \leq \delta + \frac{\eta }{3} \biggl( \delta + \sum _{i=1}^2 \sum _{j = 1}^3 (d[X^0_i;T_j] - d[X^0_i; X_i]) \biggr). \end{align*}

label: de-prop
prose: Let \(X_1, X_2\) be tau-minimizers. Then \(d[X_1;X_2] = 0\).

label: entropy-pfr
prose: Let \(G = \mathbb {F}_2^n\), and suppose that \(X^0_1, X^0_2\) are \(G\)-valued random variables. Then there is some subgroup \(H \leq G\) such that \[ d[X^0_1;U_H] + d[X^0_2;U_H] \le 11 d[X^0_1;X^0_2], \] where \(U_H\) is uniformly distributed on \(H\). Furthermore, both \(d[X^0_1;U_H]\) and \(d[X^0_2;U_H]\) are at most \(6 d[X^0_1;X^0_2]\).

label: ruz-cov
prose: If \(A,B\) are finite non-empty subsets of a group \(G\), then \(A\) can be covered by at most \(|A+B|/|B|\) translates of \(B-B\).

label: pfr_aux
prose: If \(A \subset {\bf F}_2^n\) is non-empty and \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then \(A\) can be covered by at most \(K ^{13/2}|A|^{1/2}/|H|^{1/2}\) translates of a subspace \(H\) of \({\bf F}_2^n\) with \begin{equation} \label{ah} |H|/|A| \in [K^{-11}, K^{11}]. \end{equation} 1

label: pfr
prose: If \(A \subset {\bf F}_2^n\) is non-empty and \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then \(A\) can be covered by most \(2K^{12}\) translates of a subspace \(H\) of \({\bf F}_2^n\) with \(|H| \leq |A|\).

label: pfr-cor
prose: If \(G\) is an abelian \(2\)-torsion group, \(A \subset G\) is non-empty finite, and \(|A+A| \leq K|A| \), then \(A\) can be covered by most \(2K^{12}\) translates of a finite group \(H\) of \(G\) with \(|H| \leq |A|\).

label: eta-def-new
prose: \(\eta \) is a real parameter with \(\eta {\gt} 0\).

label: construct-good-prelim-improv
prose: One has \begin{align*} k \leq \delta + \eta (& d[X^0_1;T_1|T_3]-d[X^0_1;X_1]) + \eta (d[X^0_2;T_2|T_3]-d[X^0_2;X_2]). \end{align*}

label: construct-good-improv
prose: One has \begin{align*} k & \leq \delta + \frac{\eta }{6} \sum _{i=1}^2 \sum _{1 \leq j,l \leq 3; j \neq l} (d[X^0_i;T_j|T_l] - d[X^0_i; X_i]) \end{align*}

label: averaged-construct-good
prose: One has \begin{align*} k & \leq I(U : V \, | \, S) + I(V : W \, | \, S) + I(W : U \, | \, S) + \frac{\eta }{6} \sum _{i=1}^2 \sum _{A,B \in \{ U,V,W\} : A \neq B} (d[X^0_i;A|B,S] - d[X^0_i; X_i]). \end{align*}

label: gen-ineq
prose: Let \(X_1, X_2, X_3, X_4\) be independent \(G\)-valued random variables, and let \(Y\) be another \(G\)-valued random variable. Set \(S := X_1+X_2+X_3+X_4\). Then \begin{align*} & d[Y; X_1+X_2|X_1 + X_3, S] - d[Y; X_1] \\ & \quad \leq \tfrac {1}{4} (d[X_1;X_2] + 2d[X_1;X_3] + d[X_2;X_4])\\ & \qquad \qquad + \tfrac {1}{4} (d[X_1|X_1+X_3;X_2|X_2+X_4] - d[X_3|X_3+X_4; X_1|X_1+X_2])\\ & \qquad \qquad + \tfrac {1}{8} (\mathbb {H}[X_1+X_2] - \mathbb {H}[X_3+X_4] + \mathbb {H}[X_2] - \mathbb {H}[X_3]\\ & \qquad \qquad \qquad + \mathbb {H}[X_2|X_2+X_4] - \mathbb {H}[X_1|X_1+X_3]). \end{align*}

label: dist-diff-bound
prose: We have \begin{align*} & \sum _{i=1}^2 \sum _{A,B \in \{ U,V,W\} : A \neq B} d[X_i^0;A|B, S] - d[X_i^0;X_i]\\ & \qquad \leq 12 k + \frac{4(2 \eta k - I_1)}{1-\eta }. \end{align*}

label: de-prop-improv
prose: Suppose \(0 {\lt} \eta {\lt} 1/8\). Let \(X_1, X_2\) be tau-minimizers. Then \(d[X_1;X_2] = 0\).

label: de-prop-lim-improv
prose: For \(\eta = 1/8\), there exist tau-minimizers \(X_1, X_2\) satisfying \(d[X_1;X_2] = 0\).

label: entropy-pfr-improv
prose: Let \(G = \mathbb {F}_2^n\), and suppose that \(X^0_1, X^0_2\) are \(G\)-valued random variables. Then there is some subgroup \(H \leq G\) such that \[ d[X^0_1;U_H] + d[X^0_2;U_H] \le 10 d[X^0_1;X^0_2], \] where \(U_H\) is uniformly distributed on \(H\). Furthermore, both \(d[X^0_1;U_H]\) and \(d[X^0_2;U_H]\) are at most \(6 d[X^0_1;X^0_2]\).

label: pfr_aux-improv
prose: If \(A \subset {\bf F}_2^n\) is non-empty and \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then \(A\) can be covered by at most \(K^6 |A|^{1/2}/|H|^{1/2}\) translates of a subspace \(H\) of \({\bf F}_2^n\) with \[ |H|/|A| \in [K^{-10}, K^{10}]. \]

label: pfr-improv
prose: If \(A \subset {\bf F}_2^n\) is non-empty and \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then \(A\) can be covered by most \(2K^{11}\) translates of a subspace \(H\) of \({\bf F}_2^n\) with \(|H| \leq |A|\).

label: hb-thm
prose: Let \(H_0\) be a subgroup of \(G\). Then every homomorphism \(\phi : H_0 \to G'\) can be extended to a homomorphism \(\tilde\phi : G \to G'\).

label: goursat
prose: Let \(H\) be a subgroup of \(G \times G'\). Then there exists a subgroup \(H_0\) of \(G\), a subgroup \(H_1\) of \(G'\), and a homomorphism \(\phi : G \to G'\) such that \[ H := \{ (x, \phi (x) + y): x \in H_0, y \in H_1 \} . \] In particular, \(|H| = |H_0| |H_1|\).

label: hom-pfr
prose: Let \(f: G \to G'\) be a function, and let \(S\) denote the set \[ S := \{ f(x+y)-f(x)-f(y): x,y \in G \} . \] Then there exists a homomorphism \(\phi : G \to G'\) such that \[ |\{ f(x) - \phi (x): x \in G \} | \leq |S|^{10}. \]

label: energy-def
prose: If \(G\) is a group, and \(A\) is a finite subset of \(G\), the additive energy \(E(A)\) of \(A\) is the number of quadruples \((a_1,a_2,a_3,a_4) \in A^4\) such that \(a_1+a_2 = a_3+a_4\).

label: cs-bound
prose: If \(G\) is a group, \(A,B\) are finite subsets of \(G\), then \[ E(A) \geq \frac{|\{ (a,a') \in A \times A: a+a' \in B \} |^2}{|B|}. \]

label: bsg
prose: Let \(G\) be an abelian group, and let \(A\) be a finite non-empty set with \(E(A) \geq |A|^3 / K\) for some \(K \geq 1\). Then there is a subset \(A'\) of \(A\) with \(|A'| \geq |A| / (C_1 K^{C_2})\) and \(|A'-A'| \leq C_3 K^{C_4} |A|\), where (provisionally) \[ C_1 = 2^4, C_2 = 1, C_3 = 2^{10}, C_4 = 5. \]

label: approx-hom-pfr
prose: Let \(G,G'\) be finite abelian \(2\)-groups. Let \(f: G \to G'\) be a function, and suppose that there are at least \(|G|^2 / K\) pairs \((x,y) \in G^2\) such that \[ f(x+y) = f(x) + f(y). \] Then there exists a homomorphism \(\phi : G \to G'\) and a constant \(c \in G'\) such that \(f(x) = \phi (x)+c\) for at least \(|G| / (2 ^{144} * K ^{122})\) values of \(x \in G\).

label: gdual
prose: Let \(G\) be a finite abelian \(2\)-group. Then the finite abelian \(2\)-group \(\mathrm{Hom}(G,\mathbb {Z}/2\mathbb {Z})\) of homomorphisms from \(G\) to \(\mathbb {Z}/2\mathbb {Z}\) has the same order as \(G\).

label: gcount
prose: Let \(G\) be a finite abelian \(2\)-group, and let \(x \in G\) be non-zero. Then there are \(|G|/2\) homomorphisms \(\phi : G \to \mathbb {Z}/2\mathbb {Z}\) such that \(\phi (x) = 1\).

label: gslice
prose: Let \(G\) be a finite abelian \(2\)-group, and let \(A\) be a subset of \(G\). Then there exists a homomorphism \(\phi : G \to \mathbb {Z}/2\mathbb {Z}\) such that \(|A \cap \phi ^{-1}(1)| \geq (|A|-1)/2\).

label: approx-hom-pfr-no-const
prose: Let \(G,G'\) be finite abelian \(2\)-groups. Let \(f: G \to G'\) be a function, and suppose that there are at least \(|G|^2 / K\) pairs \((x,y) \in G^2\) such that \[ f(x+y) = f(x) + f(y). \] Then there exists a homomorphism \(\phi '': G \to G'\) such that \(f(x) = \phi ''(x)\) for at least \((|G| / (2 ^{172} * K ^{146}) - 1)/2\) values of \(x \in G\).

label: torsion-free-doubling
prose: If \(G\) is torsion-free and \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables then \(d[X;2Y]\leq 5d[X;Y]\).

label: torsion-dist-shrinking
prose: If \(G\) is a torsion-free group and \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables and \(\phi :G\to \mathbb {F}_2^d\) is a homomorphism then \[ \mathbb {H}(\phi (X))\leq 10d[X;Y]. \]

label: app-ent-pfr
prose: Let \(G=\mathbb {F}_2^n\) and \(\alpha \in (0,1)\) and let \(X,Y\) be \(G\)-valued random variables such that \[ \mathbb {H}(X)+\mathbb {H}(Y){\gt} \frac{20}{\alpha } d[X;Y]. \] There is a non-trivial subgroup \(H\leq G\) such that \[ \log \lvert H\rvert {\lt}\frac{1+\alpha }{2}(\mathbb {H}(X)+\mathbb {H}(Y)) \] and \[ \mathbb {H}(\psi (X))+\mathbb {H}(\psi (Y)){\lt} \alpha (\mathbb {H}(X)+\mathbb {H}(Y)) \] where \(\psi :G\to G/H\) is the natural projection homomorphism.

label: pfr-projection'
prose: If \(G=\mathbb {F}_2^d\) and \(\alpha \in (0,1)\) and \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables then there is a subgroup \(H\leq \mathbb {F}_2^d\) such that \[ \log \lvert H\rvert \leq \frac{1+\alpha }{2(1-\alpha )} (\mathbb {H}(X)+\mathbb {H}(Y)) \] and if \(\psi :G \to G/H\) is the natural projection then \[ \mathbb {H}(\psi (X))+\mathbb {H}(\psi (Y))\leq \frac{20}{\alpha } d[\psi (X);\psi (Y)]. \]

label: pfr-projection
prose: If \(G=\mathbb {F}_2^d\) and \(\alpha \in (0,1)\) and \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued random variables then there is a subgroup \(H\leq \mathbb {F}_2^d\) such that \[ \log \lvert H\rvert \leq 2 (\mathbb {H}(X)+\mathbb {H}(Y)) \] and if \(\psi :G \to G/H\) is the natural projection then \[ \mathbb {H}(\psi (X))+\mathbb {H}(\psi (Y))\leq 34 d[\psi (X);\psi (Y)]. \]

label: single-fibres
prose: Let \(\phi :G\to H\) be a homomorphism and \(A,B\subseteq G\) be finite subsets. If \(x,y\in H\) then let \(A_x=A\cap \phi ^{-1}(x)\) and \(B_y=B\cap \phi ^{-1}(y)\). There exist \(x,y\in H\) such that \(A_x,B_y\) are both non-empty and \[ d[\phi (U_A);\phi (U_B)]\log \frac{\lvert A\rvert \lvert B\rvert }{\lvert A_x\rvert \lvert B_y\rvert }\leq (\mathbb {H}(\phi (U_A))+\mathbb {H}(\phi (U_B)))(d(U_A,U_B)-d(U_{A_x},U_{B_y})). \]

label: dimension-def
prose: If \(A\subseteq \mathbb {Z}^{d}\) then by \(\dim (A)\) we mean the dimension of the span of \(A-A\) over the reals – equivalently, the smallest \(d'\) such that \(A\) lies in a coset of a subgroup isomorphic to \(\mathbb {Z}^{d'}\).

label: weak-pfr-asymm
prose: If \(A,B\subseteq \mathbb {Z}^d\) are finite non-empty sets then there exist non-empty \(A'\subseteq A\) and \(B'\subseteq B\) such that \[ \log \frac{\lvert A\rvert \lvert B\rvert }{\lvert A'\rvert \lvert B'\rvert }\leq 34d[U_A;U_B] \] such that \(\max (\dim A',\dim B')\leq \frac{40}{\log 2} d[U_A;U_B]\).

label: weak-pfr-symm
prose: If \(A\subseteq \mathbb {Z}^d\) is a finite non-empty set with \(d[U_A;U_A]\leq \log K\) then there exists a non-empty \(A'\subseteq A\) such that \[ \lvert A'\rvert \geq K^{-17}\lvert A\rvert \] and \(\dim A'\leq \frac{40}{\log 2} \log K\).

label: weak-pfr-int
prose: Let \(A\subseteq \mathbb {Z}^d\) and \(\lvert A-A\rvert \leq K\lvert A\rvert \). There exists \(A'\subseteq A\) such that \(\lvert A'\rvert \geq K^{-17}\lvert A\rvert \) and \(\dim A' \leq \frac{40}{\log 2}\log K\).

label: data-process-single
prose: Let \(X\) be a random variable. Then for any function \(f\) on the range of \(X\), one has \(\mathbb {H}[f(X)] \leq \mathbb {H}[X]\).

label: data-process-unc-one
prose: Let \(X,Y\) be random variables. For any function \(f, g\) on the range of \(X\), we have \(\mathbb {I}[f(X) : Y] \leq \mathbb {I}[X:Y]\).

label: data-process-unc
prose: Let \(X,Y\) be random variables. For any functions \(f, g\) on the ranges of \(X, Y\) respectively, we have \(\mathbb {I}[f(X) : g(Y )] \leq \mathbb {I}[X : Y]\).

label: data-process
prose: Let \(X,Y,Z\). For any functions \(f, g\) on the ranges of \(X, Y\) respectively, we have \(\mathbb {I}[f(X) : g(Y )|Z] \leq \mathbb {I}[X :Y |Z]\).

label: sign-flip
prose: If \(X,Y\) are \(G\)-valued, then \[ d[X ; -Y] \leq 3 d[X;Y]. \]

label: klm-1
prose: If \(n \geq 0\) and \(X, Y_1, \dots , Y_n\) are jointly independent \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ \mathbb {H}\left[X + \sum _{i=1}^n Y_i\right] - \mathbb {H}[X] \leq \sum _{i=1}^n \left(\mathbb {H}[X+Y_i] - \mathbb {H}[X]\right). \]

label: klm-2
prose: If \(n \geq 1\) and \(X, Y_1, \dots , Y_n\) are jointly independent \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ d[X; \sum _{i=1}^n Y_i] \leq 2 \sum _{i=1}^n d[X; Y_i]. \]

label: klm-3
prose: If \(n \geq 1\) and \(X, Y_1, \dots , Y_n\) are jointly independent \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ d\left[X; \sum _{i=1}^n Y_i\right] \leq d\left[X; Y_1\right] + \frac{1}{2}\left(\mathbb {H}\left[ \sum _{i=1}^n Y_i\right] - \mathbb {H}[Y_1]\right). \]

label: compare-sums
prose: Let \((X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) and \((Y_j)_{1 \leq j \leq l}\) be tuples of jointly independent random variables (so the \(X\)’s and \(Y\)’s are also independent of each other), and let \(f: \{ 1,\dots ,l\} \to \{ 1,\dots ,m\} \) be a function, then \[ \mathbb {H}[\sum _{j=1}^l Y_j] \leq \mathbb {H}[ \sum _{i=1}^m X_i ] + \sum _{j=1}^l (\mathbb {H}[ Y_j - X_{f(j)}] - \mathbb {H}[X_{f(j)}]). \]

label: sum-dilate-I
prose: Let \(X,Y,X'\) be independent \(G\)-valued random variables, with \(X'\) a copy of \(X\), and let \(a\) be an integer. Then \[ \mathbb {H}[X-(a+1)Y] \leq \mathbb {H}[X-aY] + \mathbb {H}[X-Y-X'] - \mathbb {H}[X] \] and \[ \mathbb {H}[X-(a-1)Y] \leq \mathbb {H}[X-aY] + \mathbb {H}[X-Y-X'] - \mathbb {H}[X]. \]

label: sum-dilate-II
prose: Let \(X,Y\) be independent \(G\)-valued random variables, and let \(a\) be an integer. Then \[ \mathbb {H}[X-aY] - \mathbb {H}[X] \leq 4 |a| d[X;Y]. \]

label: multidist-def
prose: Let \(m\) be a positive integer, and let \(X_{[m]} = (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) be an \(m\)-tuple of \(G\)-valued random variables \(X_i\). Then we define \[ D[X_{[m]}] := \mathbb {H}[\sum _{i=1}^m \tilde X_i] - \frac{1}{m} \sum _{i=1}^m \mathbb {H}[\tilde X_i], \] where the \(\tilde X_i\) are independent copies of the \(X_i\).

label: multidist-copy
prose: If \(X_{[m]} = (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) and \(Y_{[m]} = (Y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) are such that \(X_i\) and \(Y_i\) have the same distribution for each \(i\), then \(D[X_{[m]}] = D[Y_{[m]}]\).

label: multidist-indep
prose: If \(X_{[m]} = (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) are jointly independent, then \(D[X_{[m]}] = \mathbb {H}[\sum _{i=1}^m X_i] - \frac{1}{m} \sum _{i=1}^m \mathbb {H}[X_i]\).

label: multidist-nonneg
prose: For any such tuple, we have \(D[X_{[m]}] \geq 0\).

label: multidist-perm
prose: If \(\phi : \{ 1,\dots ,m\} \to \{ 1,\dots ,m\} \) is a bijection, then \(D[X_{[m]}] = D[(X_{\phi (j)})_{1 \leq j \leq m}]\).

label: multidist-ruzsa-I
prose: Let \(m \ge 2\), and let \(X_{[m]}\) be a tuple of \(G\)-valued random variables. Then \[ \sum _{1 \leq j,k \leq m: j \neq k} d[X_j; -X_k] \leq m(m-1) D[X_{[m]}]. \]

label: multidist-ruzsa-II
prose: Let \(m \ge 2\), and let \(X_{[m]}\) be a tuple of \(G\)-valued random variables. Then \[ \sum _{j=1}^m d[X_j;X_j] \leq 2 m D[X_{[m]}]. \]

label: multidist-ruzsa-III
prose: Let \(m \ge 2\), and let \(X_{[m]}\) be a tuple of \(G\)-valued random variables. If the \(X_i\) all have the same distribution, then \(D[X_{[m]}] \leq m d[X_i;X_i]\) for any \(1 \leq i \leq m\).

label: multidist-ruzsa-IV
prose: Let \(m \ge 2\), and let \(X_{[m]}\) be a tuple of independent \(G\)-valued random variables. Let \(W := \sum _{i=1}^m X_i\). Then \[ d[W;-W] \leq 2 D[X_i]. \]

label: multi-zero
prose: If \(D[X_{[m]}]=0\), then for each \(1 \leq i \leq m\) there is a finite subgroup \(H_i \leq G\) such that \(d[X_i; U_{H_i}] = 0\).

label: eta-def-multi
prose: We set \(\eta := \frac{1}{32m^3}\).

label: tau-def-multi
prose: If \((X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) is a tuple, we define its \(\tau \)-functional \[ \tau [ (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] := D[(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] + \eta \sum _{i=1}^m d[X_i; X^0]. \]

label: tau-min-multi
prose: A \(\tau \)-minimizer is a tuple \((X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) that minimizes the \(\tau \)-functional among all tuples of \(G\)-valued random variables.

label: tau-min-exist-multi
prose: If \(G\) is finite, then a \(\tau \)-minimizer exists.

label: tau-ref
prose: If \((X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) is a \(\tau \)-minimizer, then \(\sum _{i=1}^m d[X_i; X^0] \leq \frac{2m}{\eta } d[X^0; X^0]\).

label: multidist-lower
prose: If \((X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) is a \(\tau \)-minimizer, and \(k := D[(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}]\), then for any other tuple \((X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\), one has \[ k - D[(X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] \leq \eta \sum _{i=1}^m d[X_i; X'_i]. \]

label: cond-multidist-def
prose: If \(X_{[m]} = (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) and \(Y_{[m]} = (Y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) are tuples of random variables, with the \(X_i\) being \(G\)-valued (but the \(Y_i\) need not be), then we define \begin{equation} \label{multi-def-cond-alt} D[ X_{[m]} | Y_{[m]} ] = \sum _{(y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}} \biggl(\prod _{1 \leq i \leq m} p_{Y_i}(y_i)\biggr) D[ (X_i \, |\, Y_i \mathop{=}y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] \end{equation} 2 where each \(y_i\) ranges over the support of \(p_{Y_i}\) for \(1 \leq i \leq m\).

label: cond-multidist-alt
prose: If the \((X_i,Y_i)\) are independent, \begin{equation} \label{multi-def-cond} D[ X_{[m]} | Y_{[m]}] := \mathbb {H}[\sum _{i=1}^m X_i \big| (Y_j)_{1 \leq j \leq m} ] - \frac{1}{m} \sum _{i=1}^m \mathbb {H}[ X_i | Y_i]. \end{equation} 3

label: cond-multidist-nonneg
prose: If \(X_{[m]} = (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) and \(Y_{[m]} = (Y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) are tuples of random variables, then \(D[ X_{[m]} | Y_{[m]} ] \geq 0\).

label: cond-multidist-lower
prose: If \((X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) is a \(\tau \)-minimizer, and \(k := D[(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}]\), then for any other tuples \((X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) and \((Y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) with the \(X'_i\) \(G\)-valued, one has \[ k - D[(X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m} | (Y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] \leq \eta \sum _{i=1}^m d[X_i; X'_i|Y_i]. \]

label: cond-multidist-lower-II
prose: With the notation of the previous lemma, we have \begin{equation} \label{5.3-conv} k - D[ X'_{[m]} | Y_{[m]} ] \leq \eta \sum _{i=1}^m d[X_{\sigma (i)};X'_i|Y_i] \end{equation} 4 for any permutation \(\sigma : \{ 1,\dots ,m\} \rightarrow \{ 1,\dots ,m\} \).

label: multidist-chain-rule
prose: Let \(\pi \colon G \to H\) be a homomorphism of abelian groups and let \(X_{[m]}\) be a tuple of jointly independent \(G\)-valued random variables. Then \(D[X_{[m]}]\) is equal to \begin{equation} D[ X_{[m]} | \pi (X_{[m]}) ] +D[ \pi (X_{[m]}) ] + \mathbb {I}[ \sum _{i=1}^m X_i : \pi (X_{[m]}) \; \big| \; \pi \bigl(\sum _{i=1}^m X_i\bigr) ] \label{chain-eq} \end{equation} 5 where \(\pi (X_{[m]}) := (\pi (X_i))_{1 \leq i \leq m}\).

label: multidist-chain-rule-cond
prose: Let \(\pi \colon G \to H\) be a homomorphism of abelian groups. Let \(I\) be a finite index set and let \(X_{[m]}\) be a tuple of \(G\)-valued random variables. Let \(Y_{[m]}\) be another tuple of random variables (not necessarily \(G\)-valued). Suppose that the pairs \((X_i, Y_i)\) are jointly independent of one another (but \(X_i\) need not be independent of \(Y_i\)). Then \begin{align} \nonumber D[ X_{[m]} | Y_{[m]} ] & = D[ X_{[m]} \, |\, \pi (X_{[m]}), Y_{[m]}] + D[ \pi (X_{[m]}) \, |\, Y_{[m]}] \\ & \quad \qquad + \mathbb {I}[ \sum _{i=1}^m X_i : \pi (X_{[m]}) \; \big| \; \pi \bigl(\sum _{i=1}^m X_i \bigr), Y_{[m]} ].\label{chain-eq-cond} \end{align}

label: multidist-chain-rule-iter
prose: Let \(m\) be a positive integer. Suppose one has a sequence \begin{equation} \label{g-seq} G_m \to G_{m-1} \to \dots \to G_1 \to G_0 = \{ 0\} \end{equation} 9 of homomorphisms between abelian groups \(G_0,\dots ,G_m\), and for each \(d=0,\dots ,m\), let \(\pi _d : G_m \to G_d\) be the homomorphism from \(G_m\) to \(G_d\) arising from this sequence by composition (so for instance \(\pi _m\) is the identity homomorphism and \(\pi _0\) is the zero homomorphism). Let \(X_{[m]} = (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}\) be a jointly independent tuple of \(G_m\)-valued random variables. Then \begin{equation} \begin{split} D[ X_{[m]} ] & = \sum _{d=1}^m D[ \pi _d(X_{[m]}) \, |\, \pi _{d-1}(X_{[m]})] \\ & \quad + \sum _{d=1}^{m-1} \mathbb {I}[ \sum _i X_i : \pi _d(X_{[m]}) \; \big| \; \pi _d\big(\sum _i X_i\big), \pi _{d-1}(X_{[m]}) ]. \end{split}\label{chain-eq-cond'} \end{equation} 10 In particular, by Lemma 2.27 , \begin{align} \nonumber D[ X_{[m]} ] \geq & \sum _{d=1}^m D[ \pi _d(X_{[m]})|\pi _{d-1}(X_{[m]}) ] \\ & + \mathbb {I}[ \sum _i X_i : \pi _1(X_{[m]}) \; \big| \; \pi _1\bigl(\sum _i X_i\bigr) ].\label{chain-eq-cond''} \end{align}

label: cor-multid
prose: Let \(G\) be an abelian group and let \(m \geq 2\). Suppose that \(X_{i,j}\), \(1 \leq i, j \leq m\), are independent \(G\)-valued random variables. Then \begin{align*} & \mathbb {I}[ \bigl(\sum _{i=1}^m X_{i,j}\bigr)_{j =1}^{m} : \bigl(\sum _{j=1}^m X_{i,j}\bigr)_{i = 1}^m \; \big| \; \sum _{i=1}^m \sum _{j = 1}^m X_{i,j} ] \\ & \quad \leq \sum _{j=1}^{m-1} \Bigl(D[(X_{i, j})_{i = 1}^m] - D[ (X_{i, j})_{i = 1}^m \; \big| \; (X_{i,j} + \cdots + X_{i,m})_{i =1}^m ]\Bigr) \\ & \qquad \qquad \qquad \qquad + D[(X_{i,m})_{i=1}^m] - D[ \bigl(\sum _{j=1}^m X_{i,j}\bigr)_{i=1}^m ], \end{align*} where all the multidistances here involve the indexing set \(\{ 1,\dots , m\} \).

label: key
prose: Suppose that \(X_{i,j}\), \(1 \leq i,j \leq m\), are jointly independent \(G\)-valued random variables, such that for each \(j = 1,\dots ,m\), the random variables \((X_{i,j})_{i = 1}^m\) coincide in distribution with some permutation of \(X_{[m]}\). Write \[ {\mathcal I} := \mathbb {I}[ \bigl(\sum _{i=1}^m X_{i,j}\bigr)_{j =1}^{m} : \bigl(\sum _{j=1}^m X_{i,j}\bigr)_{i = 1}^m \; \big| \; \sum _{i=1}^m \sum _{j = 1}^m X_{i,j} ]. \] Then \begin{equation} \label{I-ineq} {\mathcal I} \leq m(4m+1) \eta k. \end{equation} 14

label: more-random
prose: By a slight abuse of notation, we identify \(\mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}\) and \(\{ 1,\dots ,m\} \) in the obvious way, and let \(Y_{i,j}\) be an independent copy of \(X_i\) for \(i,j \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}\). Then also define: \[ W := \sum _{i,j \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}} Y_{i,j} \] and \[ Z_1 := \sum _{i,j \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}} i Y_{i,j},\ \ Z_2 := \sum _{i,j \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}} j Y_{i,j},\ \ Z_3 := \sum _{i,j \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}} (-i-j) Y_{i,j}. \] The addition \((-i-j)\) takes place over \(\mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}\). Note that, because we are assuming \(G\) is \(m\)-torsion, it is well-defined to multiply elements of \(G\) by elements of \(\mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}\). We will also define for \(i,j,r \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}\) the variables \begin{equation} \label{pqr-defs} P_i := \sum _{j \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}} Y_{i,j} , \quad Q_j := \sum _{i \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}} Y_{i,j} , \quad R_r := \sum _{\substack {i,j \in \mathbb {Z}/m\mathbb {Z}\\ i+j=-r}} Y_{i,j} .\end{equation} 21

label: Zero-sum
prose: We have \begin{equation} \label{eq:sum-zero} Z_1+Z_2+Z_3= 0 \end{equation} 22

label: prop:52
prose: We have \[ \mathbb {I}[Z_1 : Z_2\, |\, W], \mathbb {I}[Z_2 : Z_3\, |\, W], \mathbb {I}[Z_1 : Z_3\, |\, W] \leq t \] where \begin{equation} \label{t-def} t := m(4m+1) \eta k. \end{equation} 23

label: ent-w
prose: We have \(\mathbb {H}[W] \leq (2m-1)k + \frac1m \sum _{i=1}^m \mathbb {H}[X_i]\).

label: ent-z2
prose: We have \(\mathbb {H}[Z_2] \leq (8m^2-16m+1) k + \frac{1}{m} \sum _{i=1}^m \mathbb {H}[X_i]\).

label: mutual-w-z2
prose: We have \(\mathbb {I}[W : Z_2] \leq 2 (m-1) k\).

label: xi-z2-w-dist
prose: We have \(\sum _{i=1}^m d[X_i;Z_2|W] \leq 4(m^3-m^2) k\).

label: lem:get-better
prose: Let \(G\) be an abelian group, let \((T_1,T_2,T_3)\) be a \(G^3\)-valued random variable such that \(T_1+T_2+T_3=0\) holds identically, and write \[ \delta := \mathbb {I}[T_1 : T_2] + \mathbb {I}[T_1 : T_3] + \mathbb {I}[T_2 : T_3]. \] Let \(Y_1,\dots ,Y_n\) be some further \(G\)-valued random variables and let \(\alpha {\gt}0\) be a constant. Then there exists a random variable \(U\) such that \begin{equation} \label{eq:get-better} d[U;U] + \alpha \sum _{i=1}^n d[Y_i;U] \leq \Bigl(2 + \frac{\alpha n}{2} \Bigr) \delta + \alpha \sum _{i=1}^n d[Y_i;T_2]. \end{equation} 27

label: k-vanish
prose: We have \(k = 0\).

label: main-entropy
prose: Suppose that \(G\) is a finite abelian group of torsion \(m\). Suppose that \(X\) is a \(G\)-valued random variable. Then there exists a subgroup \(H \leq G\) such that \[ d[X;U_H] \leq 64 m^3 d[X;X]. \]

label: pfr_aux_torsion
prose: Suppose that \(G\) is a finite abelian group of torsion \(m\). If \(A \subset G\) is non-empty and \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then \(A\) can be covered by at most \(K ^{128m^3+1}|A|^{1/2}/|H|^{1/2}\) translates of a subspace \(H\) of \(G\) with \begin{equation} \label{ah2} |H|/|A| \in [K^{-256m^3}, K^{256m^3}]. \end{equation} 36

label: pfr-torsion
prose: Suppose that \(G\) is a finite abelian group of torsion \(m\). If \(A \subset G\) is non-empty and \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then \(A\) can be covered by most \(mK^{256m^3+1}\) translates of a subspace \(H\) of \(G\) with \(|H| \leq |A|\).

label: kl-div
prose: If \(X,Y\) are two \(G\)-valued random variables, the Kullback–Leibler divergence is defined as \[ D_{KL}(X\Vert Y) := \sum _x \mathbf{P}(X=x) \log \frac{\mathbf{P}(X=x)}{\mathbf{P}(Y=x)}. \]

label: kl-div-copy
prose: If \(X'\) is a copy of \(X\), and \(Y'\) is a copy of \(Y\), then \(D_{KL}(X'\Vert Y') = D_{KL}(X\Vert Y)\).

label: Gibbs
prose: \(D_{KL}(X\Vert Y) \geq 0\).

label: Gibbs-converse
prose: If \(D_{KL}(X\Vert Y) = 0\), then \(Y\) is a copy of \(X\).

label: kl-div-convex
prose: If \(S\) is a finite set, \(\sum _{s \in S} w_s = 1\) for some non-negative \(w_s\), and \({\bf P}(X=x) = \sum _{s\in S} w_s {\bf P}(X_s=x)\), \({\bf P}(Y=x) = \sum _{s\in S} w_s {\bf P}(Y_s=x)\) for all \(x\), then \[ D_{KL}(X\Vert Y) \le \sum _{s\in S} w_s D_{KL}(X_s\Vert Y_s). \]

label: kl-div-inj
prose: If \(f:G \to H\) is an injection, then \(D_{KL}(f(X)\Vert f(Y)) = D_{KL}(X\Vert Y)\).

label: kl-sums
prose: If \(X, Y, Z\) are independent \(G\)-valued random variables, then \[ D_{KL}(X+Z\Vert Y+Z) \leq D_{KL}(X\Vert Y). \]

label: ckl-div
prose: If \(X,Y,Z\) are random variables, with \(X,Z\) defined on the same sample space, we define \[ D_{KL}(X|Z \Vert Y) := \sum _z \mathbf{P}(Z=z) D_{KL}( (X|Z=z) \Vert Y). \]

label: kl-cond
prose: If \(X, Y\) are independent \(G\)-valued random variables, and \(Z\) is another random variable defined on the same sample space as \(X\), then \[ D_{KL}((X|Z)\Vert Y) = D_{KL}(X\Vert Y) + \mathbb {H}[X] - \mathbb {H}[X|Z]. \]

label: Conditional-Gibbs
prose: \(D_{KL}((X|W)\Vert Y) \geq 0\).

label: rhominus-def
prose: For any \(G\)-valued random variable \(X\), we define \(\rho ^-(X)\) to be the infimum of \(D_{KL}(X \Vert U_A + T)\), where \(U_A\) is uniform on \(A\) and \(T\) ranges over \(G\)-valued random variables independent of \(U_A\).

label: rhoplus-def
prose: For any \(G\)-valued random variable \(X\), we define \(\rho ^+(X) := \rho ^-(X) + \mathbb {H}(X) - \mathbb {H}(U_A)\).

label: rhoMinus_nonneg
prose: We have \(\rho ^-(X) \geq 0\).

label: rhominus-subgroup
prose: If \(H\) is a finite subgroup of \(G\), then \(\rho ^-(U_H) = \log |A| - \log \max _t |A \cap (H+t)|\).

label: rhoplus-subgroup
prose: If \(H\) is a finite subgroup of \(G\), then \(\rho ^+(U_H) = \log |H| - \log \max _t |A \cap (H+t)|\).

label: rho-def
prose: We define \(\rho (X) := (\rho ^+(X) + \rho ^-(X))/2\).

label: rho_of_uniform
prose: We have \(\rho (U_A) = 0\).

label: rho-subgroup
prose: If \(H\) is a finite subgroup of \(G\), and \(\rho (U_H) \leq r\), then there exists \(t\) such that \(|A \cap (H+t)| \geq e^{-r} \sqrt{|A||H|}\), and \(|H|/|A|\in [e^{-2r},e^{2r}]\).

label: rho-invariant
prose: For any \(s \in G\), \(\rho (X+s) = \rho (X)\).

label: rho-cts
prose: \(\rho (X)\) depends continuously on the distribution of \(X\).

label: rho-sums
prose: If \(X,Y\) are independent, one has \[ \rho ^-(X+Y) \leq \rho ^-(X) \] \[ \rho ^+(X+Y) \leq \rho ^+(X) + \mathbb {H}[X+Y] - \mathbb {H}[X] \] and \[ \rho (X+Y) \leq \rho (X) + \frac{1}{2}( \mathbb {H}[X+Y] - \mathbb {H}[X] ). \]

label: rho-cond-def
prose: We define \(\rho (X|Y) := \sum _y {\bf P}(Y=y) \rho (X|Y=y)\).

label: rho-cond-invariant
prose: For any \(s\in G\), \(\rho (X+s|Y)=\rho (X|Y)\).

label: rho-cond-relabeled
prose: If \(f\) is injective, then \(\rho (X|f(Y))=\rho (X|Y)\).

label: rho-cond
prose: If \(X,Z\) are defined on the same space, one has \[ \rho ^-(X|Z) \leq \rho ^-(X) + \mathbb {H}[X] - \mathbb {H}[X|Z] \] \[ \rho ^+(X|Z) \leq \rho ^+(X) \] and \[ \rho (X|Z) \leq \rho (X) + \frac{1}{2}( \mathbb {H}[X] - \mathbb {H}[X|Z] ). \]

label: rho-sums-sym
prose: If \(X,Y\) are independent, then \[ \rho (X+Y) \leq \frac{1}{2}(\rho (X)+\rho (Y) + d[X;Y]). \]

label: rho-cond-sym
prose: If \(X,Y\) are independent, then \[ \rho (X | X+Y) \leq \frac{1}{2}(\rho (X)+\rho (Y) + d[X;Y]). \]

label: phi-min-def
prose: Given \(G\)-valued random variables \(X,Y\), define \[ \phi [X;Y] := d[X;Y] + \eta (\rho (X) + \rho (Y)) \] and define a \(\phi \)-minimizer to be a pair of random variables \(X,Y\) which minimizes \(\phi [X;Y]\).

label: phi-min-exist
prose: There exists a \(\phi \)-minimizer.

label: rho-BSG-triplet
prose: If \(G\)-valued random variables \(T_1,T_2,T_3\) satisfy \(T_1+T_2+T_3=0\), then \[ d[X_1;X_2]\le 3\mathbb {I}[T_1:T_2] + (2\mathbb {H}[T_3]-\mathbb {H}[T_1]-\mathbb {H}[T_2])+ \eta (\rho (T_1|T_3)+\rho (T_2|T_3)-\rho (X_1)-\rho (X_2)). \]

label: rho-BSG-triplet-symmetrized
prose: If \(G\)-valued random variables \(T_1,T_2,T_3\) satisfy \(T_1+T_2+T_3=0\), then \[ d[X_1;X_2] \leq \sum _{1 \leq i{\lt}j \leq 3} \mathbb {I}[T_i:T_j] + \frac{\eta }{3} \sum _{1 \leq i{\lt}j \leq 3} (\rho (T_i|T_j) + \rho (T_j|T_i) -\rho (X_1)-\rho (X_2)) \]

label: rho-increase
prose: For independent random variables \(Y_1,Y_2,Y_3,Y_4\) over \(G\), define \(S:=Y_1+Y_2+Y_3+Y_4\), \(T_1:=Y_1+Y_2\), \(T_2:=Y_1+Y_3\). Then \[ \rho (T_1|T_2,S)+\rho (T_2|T_1,S) - \frac{1}{2}\sum _{i} \rho (Y_i)\le \frac{1}{2}(d[Y_1;Y_2]+d[Y_3;Y_4]+d[Y_1;Y_3]+d[Y_2;Y_4]). \]

label: rho-increase-symmetrized
prose: For independent random variables \(Y_1,Y_2,Y_3,Y_4\) over \(G\), define \(T_1:=Y_1+Y_2,T_2:=Y_1+Y_3,T_3:=Y_2+Y_3\) and \(S:=Y_1+Y_2+Y_3+Y_4\). Then \[ \sum _{1 \leq i{\lt}j \leq 3} (\rho (T_i|T_j,S) + \rho (T_j|T_i,S) - \frac{1}{2}\sum _{i} \rho (Y_i))\le \sum _{1\leq i {\lt} j \leq 4}d[Y_i;Y_j] \]

label: phi-minimizer-zero-distance
prose: If \(X_1,X_2\) is a \(\phi \)-minimizer, then \(d[X_1;X_2] = 0\).

label: pfr-rho
prose: For any random variables \(Y_1,Y_2\), there exist a subgroup \(H\) such that \[ 2\rho (U_H) \leq \rho (Y_1) + \rho (Y_2) + 8 d[Y_1;Y_2]. \]

label: pfr-9-aux
prose: If \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then there exists a subgroup \(H\) and \(t\in G\) such that \(|A \cap (H+t)| \geq K^{-4} \sqrt{|A||H|}\), and \(|H|/|A|\in [K^{-8},K^8]\).

label: pfr-9-aux'
prose: If \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then there exist a subgroup \(H\) and a subset \(c\) of \(G\) with \(A \subseteq c + H\), such that \(|c| \leq K^{5} |A|^{1/2}/|H|^{1/2}\) and \(|H|/|A|\in [K^{-8},K^8]\).

label: pfr-9
prose: If \(A \subset {\bf F}_2^n\) is finite non-empty with \(|A+A| \leq K|A|\), then there exists a subgroup \(H\) of \({\bf F}_2^n\) with \(|H| \leq |A|\) such that \(A\) can be covered by at most \(2K^9\) translates of \(H\).
